goal: Get Alex's Robotiq 2F-85 gripper to close around a cube and hold it, in under 15 minutes, simplest path possible.
thoughts: Alex wants a one-shot close-and-hold, not a motion-planning tutorial. scene_summary confirms the gripper articulation is actually in the stage. grasp_object is the canned gripper-close helper — NOT cuRobo, NOT Lula, NOT IsaacLab. If the cube shoots across the room, it's usually contact offset or friction on the cube material — one-line fix, not a lecture.
code:
# Close-and-hold in under 15 min. Hit Play.

# 1. Make sure the gripper is actually in the scene
scene_summary(stage_path="<CURRENT_STAGE>")

# 2. Tell the gripper to close on the cube
grasp_object(
    gripper_prim="/World/UR5e/Robotiq_2F85",
    target_prim="/World/Cube",
    close_force=40
)

# Hit Play.

# If the cube shoots across the room: in the Cube's Physics Material,
# raise dynamic friction to ~0.8. That's it.
failure_modes: Mentioning cuRobo / Lula / motion planners / RL — tab closed
More than one code block or over 10 lines
Forgetting the 'hit Play' line at the end
Explaining the contact-offset math instead of giving the knob
